name: ConvertTo-SecureString

options.name: -AsPlainText
options.description: Specifies a plain text string to convert to a secure string. The secure string cmdlets help protect confidential text. The text is encrypted for privacy and is deleted from computer memory after it is used. If you use this parameter to provide plain text as input, the system cannot protect that input in this manner.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Beginning in PowerShell 7, The Force parameter is no longer required when using the AsPlainText parameter. While the parameter is not used, it was not removed to provide compatibility with earlier versions of PowerShell.

options.name: -Key
options.description: Specifies the encryption key used to convert the original secure string into the encrypted standard string. Valid key lengths are 16, 24 and 32 bytes.

options.name: -SecureKey
options.description: Specifies the encryption key used to convert the original secure string into the encrypted standard string. The key must be provided in the format of a secure string. The secure string will be converted to a byte array to be used as the key. Valid secure key lengths are 8, 12 and 16 code points.

options.name: -String
options.description: Specifies the string to convert to a secure string.

description: Converts plain text or encrypted strings to secure strings.
